metadata.name: AntiVEGF-Guideline-KG
metadata.description: Lightweight neuro-symbolic clinical-guideline knowledge graph for anti-VEGF continuation decisions from OCT biomarkers. Directed labelled graph with confidence-weighted causal edges + production rules.
metadata.method_source: Wang MH, Sensors 2025, 25:6879 (neuro-symbolic + LLM AMD prognosis): ontology KG + symbolic rules + RAG-LLM narrative. Adapted from diagnosis to anti-VEGF treatment-continuation.
metadata.label_source: anti-VEGF clinical guidelines + APTOS-2021_analysis.md biomarker definitions
metadata.ontology: UMLS CUIs reused from the repo's KAD OCT-KG (experiments_product/5_kad_oct_diagnosis_executed.ipynb); SNOMED-CT/ICD-10 fields left null where not verified (avoid fabrication)
metadata.notes: Rules are intentionally few (~10) and fully public for reproducibility. weight in [0,1] encodes clinical confidence.

nodes.label: Intraretinal fluid
nodes.definition: Hyporeflective cystoid spaces within retinal layers; marker of active exudation (esp. DME).

nodes.label: Subretinal fluid
nodes.definition: Dome-shaped hyporeflective area between neurosensory retina and RPE; marker of active CNV/exudation.

nodes.label: Pigment epithelial detachment
nodes.definition: RPE separation from Bruch's membrane; associated with CNV and PCV; activity context-dependent.

nodes.label: Hyperreflective foci
nodes.definition: Small discrete hyperreflective dots; highly prevalent (95.7%), weak standalone treatment signal.

edges.head: SRF
edges.evidence: Persistent subretinal fluid indicates active exudation -> continue anti-VEGF.

edges.head: IRF
edges.evidence: Intraretinal fluid indicates active edema -> continue anti-VEGF.

edges.head: PED
edges.evidence: PED alone (no IRF/SRF) is context-dependent; serous/fibrovascular subtype matters.

edges.head: HRF
edges.evidence: HRF is highly prevalent and weakly specific; minor contribution.

rules.rationale: Subretinal fluid present -> active exudation -> continue.
Intraretinal fluid present -> active edema -> continue.
No intraretinal/subretinal fluid and no PED (dry macula) -> stop / observe.
PED only, no active fluid -> case-dependent.
